payload.body: Repro: heck-strict case study at HEAD (cold cache, freshly built wrapper) fails R0042 'call to transform must use named arguments (arity 4)' on calls that ARE fully named — e.g. transform(s: ..., with_word: |s, f| {...}, boundary: ..., f: f). The arg splitter cuts on top-level commas; a closure's |s, f| params are bare tokens (not a Group), so the comma inside the pipes splits the closure into two pseudo-args and the call no longer parses as named. Same bug class as the generics-comma fix already in named_args.rs (~line 699). Every case-study/eval source passing closures via named args is affected. Fix: treat |...| (and move |...|) as an atomic unit when splitting; mind BitOr ambiguity.
